revenue: $50.53 billion, up 9.4% YoY from $46.20 billion in Q3 2024
net_income: $2.45 billion, up 174% YoY from $892 million in Q3 2024
key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $0.60, up from $0.22 in Q3 2024
Operating income of $1.56 billion, up 77% YoY from $880 million
Cash and cash equivalents of $26.79 billion, up from $22.94 billion at year-end 2024
qoq_changes: Significant sequential surge in profitability, with Q3 generating $2.45 billion of the $2.88 billion year-to-date net income
Total assets expanded to $300.99 billion, up from $285.20 billion at the end of 2024
new_risks: Cost of sales increased by over $3.2 billion YoY to $43.41 billion, pressuring gross margins
Ford Credit interest, operating, and other expenses rose to $2.83 billion, reflecting elevated financing costs
management_tone: Bullish, driven by a massive year-over-year surge in net income and strong top-line revenue growth, though underlying cost increases warrant ongoing attention.
analyst_note: Ford delivered a highly robust Q3 2025, demonstrating excellent operating leverage as a 9.4% revenue increase translated into a 174% jump in net income. However, investors should closely monitor the rising cost of sales and Ford Credit expenses to gauge the sustainability of these margins.
